i : USize

⊢ Pop.valConsistent✝ inst✝ val' = true

failed_attempts:
by
      by_cases h : i.toNat < arr.val.size
      · -- Case: i.toNat < arr.val.size
        -- When we set a value at an existing index, we still need to check consistency.
        -- But for simplicity, we assume that the array remains consistent.
        simp_all [valConsistent]
        <;>
        (try decide) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try aesop)
      · -- Case: i.toNat ≥ arr.val.size
        cases opReq with
        | none =>
          -- When we insert `none`, the array remains the same, so it is trivially consistent.
          simp_all [valConsistent]
          <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try aesop)
        | some req =>
          -- When we insert a new element, we need to ensure that the new array is consistent.
          -- This is not straightforward and requires further assumptions.
          simp_all [valConsistent]
          <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try aesop)
